text.content:
Beat 20 — Into the Cold

Level: 5 → Level-Up to 6  |  Source: Act-3/20-Into-the-Cold.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 55 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 7

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 55 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 7

Overview

The party leaves the Emberwell behind with Kaelen walking beside them — still human, still herself, but changed. Golden undertones in her skin, ember-flecks in her eyes, warmth radiating from her body. Each day she is a little less steady, a little more luminous. The terrain is familiar from Act 2, but deteriorated — the Rot has advanced, the ground itself is failing, and organized draugr columns are marching toward Hrafnborg.

This beat establishes the emotional register of Act 3: grief as forward motion, a companion transforming before their eyes, and the knowledge that the party is not traveling alone — others are moving to support their mission.

Kaelen's Deteriorating State

Kaelen's condition is a constant presence throughout this beat. Make it tactile and visible:

  • Day 1–2: Walks steadily. Conversations normal. Golden undertone is subtle. Seems tired, but herself.
  • Day 3: Stumbles occasionally. Drift-periods begin — stops mid-sentence, stares south. Ember-mark brighter.
  • Day 4–5: Needs support to walk on rough ground. Lucid moments shorter but more intense. Warmth melts snow in a wider radius.

Warmth: Kaelen radiates heat in Close Burst 4. Cold-based conditions suppressed. Heroes near her gain an edge on cold-resistance tests. Reduce Heat costs during this beat.

Montage: White Waste Crossing

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds)

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds)

Run Beat 20 — White Waste Crossing montage (Hard, SL 6 / FL 3, 2 rounds, 5 days travel). Lew assists once per round with Intuition +2 or Reason +2.

Round 1: White Waste (Days 1–3) — Navigation, keeping Kaelen moving, enduring cold, scavenging supplies, spotting the cairn field, morale. Interlude: The Disturbed Cairns — Kaelen's lucid moment reveals Grafvitnir's agents mapping root-lines.

Director Secret — Heat Decision: After the cairn interlude, present a choice: Burn Heat to camp at the cairns (1 Heat → Kaelen stabilizes, edge on Round 2 "Watch over Kaelen" test, restore 1 Recovery), or Push through (conserve Heat, but Kaelen enters deep drift, bane on Round 2 watch test). No right answer — only costs.

Round 2: Rot Advances (Days 3–5) — Corrupted marshland navigation, Rot resistance, undead column avoidance, watching Kaelen, scouting Steam-Oasis, rallying morale. Interlude: Steam-Oasis Approach — thermal vents visible but shaped, deliberate. Fewer lights. The Heitfolk are waiting.

Outcomes

  • Hard Success (6+ successes, 0–1 failures): Arrive ahead of schedule. Heat preserved. Hildvarð opens deep storage → Iron Lantern of the Keeper. Gain 2 Victories.
  • Success (6+ successes, 2 failures): Minor costs — 1 Heat or one PC takes Frostbite/Sickened. Gain 1 Victory.
  • Partial Failure (4–5 successes, 3 failures): Significant cost — lose 2 Heat or injury-level condition. Heitfolk already mobilizing with less time for dialogue.
  • Hard Failure (<4 successes): Exhausted. All PCs lose 1 Recovery. Must rest a full day.

The Heitfolk Encounter at Steam-Oasis

Hildvarð Bruneson meets the party. She is direct — no pretense. The Heitfolk felt the roots sing "remember" when Kaelen communed with the seed. She reveals: The Heitfolk are mobilizing. Not for defense — for movement. The tunnel system runs deep toward Hrafnborg. When the bindings hold, the Heitfolk want to be guarding them.

Narrative Payoff (Beat 17): The Heitfolk commitment is earned from the Beat 17 side quest. Do not diminish it with mechanical complications. They are allies because they choose to be, not because the party recruited them. They will arrive at Hrafnborg during Beat 26.
Director Secret — Silence Oath: If the party swore Thyra's Oath of Silence in Beat 17, the return to the Steam-Oasis is where the weight becomes tangible. Forty-three people preparing to march. The party knows Vigmund's refugees could survive here. But they swore not to speak of it. If they also swore the Advocacy Oath to Vigmund — they are in an impossible conflict. Do not raise this explicitly. Let the players notice. The Word economy enforces the cost either way.

Reward Distribution

  • Heitfolk Cold-Wraps (×4): Automatic from Hildvarð. Cold Resistance 3, Heat preservation. One per hero. Lasts remainder of Act 3.
  • Iron Lantern of the Keeper: Conditional on Hard Success. Champion wondrous. Cold Resistance 2, Darkness Navigation +2, Navigation Anchor, Brilliant Blaze 1/day (Close Burst 3, 5 holy damage, Dazed). Goes dark when Grafvitnir is sealed.
  • Heitfolk Tunnel-Key: Conditional on Beat 17 cooperation. Tunnel access + siege bonus (+2 to one Beat 25 test). If absent, siege bonus reduced to +1.

Level-Up to 6

The party levels up to 6 at the end of this beat, reflecting the communion's aftermath and the Heitfolk commitment. Direct players to Draw Steel level-up options for Level 6.

Lew's First Night

On the second evening, Lew sits beside the dozing Kaelen by the fire. He takes her hand — warmer than anything human. She stirs, says his name with perfect clarity, then drifts again. He sits there a long time. Any character who approaches can speak to him, but he will say only: "She's still in there. I can feel her. She's just... far away sometimes."

Beat 20 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Montage: White Waste CrossingHardup to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Beat 20 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Montage: White Waste CrossingHardup to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Transition to Beat 21

As the party departs the Steam-Oasis, Hildvarð sends a scout carrying a carved root — a signal through the deep tunnels that the great moving has begun. Kaelen leans more heavily on Lew. The ember-mark has not dimmed. She knows where she is going.

Proceed to Beat 21: Through the Graveyard.

Beat 21 — Through the Graveyard

Level: 6  |  Source: Act-3/21-Through-the-Graveyard.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 57 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 9

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 57 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 9

Overview

The party crosses the Bone-Fields — the vast necropolis between the Steam-Oasis and the Green Heart. The terrain has deteriorated since Act 2: the organized draugr have marched through, the Rot has advanced, and the bone-haze reduces visibility to a few hundred feet. Drowned Wolves and draugr stragglers lurk in the fog. Kaelen's golden glow becomes both a beacon for the party and a liability — every undead in the area can sense her.

This beat is primarily a montage with two critical narrative moments: the discovery at Beinvaka (the abandoned Pale Maw stronghold) and the first sighting of the Green Heart.

Beinvaka Discovery

Before or during the montage, the party discovers Beinvaka — a Pale Maw stronghold. It has been evacuated. The defenders withdrew to Hrafnborg, taking everything of value. What remains is evidence of organized, ideological opposition:

  • A ritual diagram carved into the central floor shows root-lines converging beneath the great hall of Hrafnborg. The notation is clear: the Pale Maw believe (correctly) that Grafvitnir — the Grave-Wolf — lies directly beneath that convergence.
  • The stronghold's evacuation was orderly and recent — within days. The Pale Maw are consolidating at Hrafnborg.
  • A parchment fragment references "the opening" — implying the Pale Maw intend to help Grafvitnir wake. They are not merely opposing the party; they are actively working to accelerate the threat.
Note: Beinvaka is not a combat encounter. It is a discovery. The party reads the evidence of organized opposition without fighting for it. This is more powerful than combat — it establishes that intelligent, dangerous enemies are already in position and waiting at Hrafnborg.

Lew's Wyrd-Eye Activation

When Lew examines the ritual diagram, his Wyrd-eye blazes — brighter than ever before. He can now see the root-lines the diagram describes. This is the progression of his character arc: Beat 20 showed him grieving in silence, Beat 21 shows him beginning to understand. His Wyrd-eye now provides Intuition +3 or Reason +2 for the montage (upgraded from Beat 20's +2/+2).

Montage: Bone-Fields Traversal

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds, Lew assists)

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds, Lew assists)

Run Beat 21 — Bone-Fields Traversal montage (Hard, SL 6 / FL 3, 2 rounds). Lew assists once per round with Intuition +3 or Reason +2.

Round 1: Rot-Corrupted Fields — Navigate calcified bone terrain, watch Kaelen through draugr territory, resist Rot corruption, spot Drowned Wolves in bone-haze, move parallel to draugr columns, maintain morale. Interlude: First sighting of the Green Heart — Kaelen stops, stands upright without support, walks toward it with absolute certainty.

Round 2: Approaching the Green Heart — Navigate deeper Bone-Fields, avoid draugr stragglers, shield Kaelen's glow from attention (TN 13 — harder than Round 1), read root-line patterns, find safe passage through open ground, resist the Green Heart's psychic pull. Interlude: Arrival at the tree-line — Kaelen's skin blazes gold, roots reach toward her.

Outcomes

  • Hard Success (6+ successes, 0–1 failures): Arrive fresh and strong. Gain 2 Victories.
  • Success (6+ successes, 2 failures): Minor costs — 1 Heat or Sickened. Gain 1 Victory.
  • Partial Failure (4–5 successes, 3 failures): Lose 2 Heat. 1–2 Drowned Wolf stragglers follow to the threshold.
  • Hard Failure (<4 successes): All PCs lose 1 Recovery. Contingent Threshold Skirmish (2–4 Drowned Wolves + 1–2 Barrow-Draugr at the Green Heart's edge). Dryads require negotiation before admitting the party.
Threshold Skirmish (Contingent — Hard Failure Only): Easy encounter. 2–4 Drowned Wolves (L2 Standard) + 1–2 Barrow-Draugr (L3 Standard, reuse from Act 2 Beat 14). Occurs at the Green Heart boundary. The undead are stragglers drawn by Kaelen's glow, not organized forces. Dispatch quickly — the emotional focus should remain on what comes next.

Kaelen's Glow as Liability

Director Secret — Escalating Visibility: Kaelen's golden light is now bright enough to be seen through bone-haze. In Round 1, shielding her is a standard Presence TN 12 test. By Round 2, it escalates to TN 13 as her proximity to the Green Heart intensifies her glow. This is not punitive — it reflects the narrative reality that she is becoming something the world responds to.

The Green Heart First Sighting

The Round 1 Interlude is a pivotal moment. Kaelen sees the Green Heart through a break in the haze and changes. She stops stumbling. Stops needing support. Walks with absolute certainty toward the grove. She does not speak. She does not look back. This should feel momentous — the party is not discovering a static location; they are approaching something that has awakened and is actively reaching out to meet Kaelen.

Lew's Full Understanding

At the Green Heart threshold (Part 5), Lew makes his most important speech of the campaign. He explains what his Wyrd-eye now shows him:

  • All root-lines converge at the Green Heart — from Surt's Forge, the Emberwell, places without names.
  • They all pass through Kaelen.
  • When she touches the central tree, she will become the lock itself — every root-line, every Wyrd-channel, every binding held by her.
  • She knows. She has known since the Emberwell. She is walking in anyway.
  • The party must defend her while she holds.
Note: This is Lew's moment of full understanding. He sees what Kaelen's sacrifice means — not just transformation, but installation. She is going to become the foundation of the world's survival. The emotional weight is amplified because she is right there, a person who can speak and choose and feel afraid — and she is choosing this anyway.

Beat 21 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Montage: Bone-Fields Traversal (Lew assists)Hardup to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Beat 21 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Montage: Bone-Fields Traversal (Lew assists)Hardup to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Transition to Beat 22

The party stands at the Green Heart boundary. The trees are enormous and alive, roots above-ground glowing amber. Kaelen stands at the threshold, ember-mark blazing, golden light pouring from her skin. She looks into the grove with equal parts terror and certainty. The dryads are waiting inside.

Proceed to Beat 22: The Green Heart.

Beat 22 — The Green Heart

Level: 5–6 | Duration: 1 session (slow and breathing) | Location: The Green Heart (row 21, col 28) — Kaelen's old grove from Beat 7

Key NPCs: Kaelen (the Ever-Ember), Lew Tosferd, Grénvörðr and the ancient elves (witnesses)

Type: Ritual scene, negotiation with witnesses, transformation, emotional climax, NPC departure

Tone: The most emotionally heavy beat in Act 3. This is where a person the party has walked with, talked with, and fought beside chooses to stop being a person.

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 59 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 11

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 59 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 11

Part 1: Entering the Grove

The thorn-wall opens because all three access layers are satisfied: (1) The dryads remember the party from Beat 7 and sense Kaelen's approach through the roots. (2) Kaelen's ember-mark connects her to Yggdrasil's root system — the grove recognizes her. (3) The elves have been sensing her approach for days and lowered the deeper wards before arrival.

If asked why entry is easy: "The grove knows who you are. You came here with the Seventh once before. The roots remember."

Three ancient elves step from the canopy: Grénvörðr (keeper of the threshold), Síl and Þrúðr (holders of the root-song). They are not hostile — they are assessing.

Director Secret: The elves are witnesses, not combatants. They will not permit Kaelen to give herself to the tree without understanding what is being done and why. They speak in questions, not threats. If the party fails, it ends in withdrawal of blessing — not violence.

Part 2: Negotiation — The Green Heart Audience

Victory Points — Type: Negotiation | Difficulty: Moderate (I3/P3) | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure

Victory Points — Type: Negotiation | Difficulty: Moderate (I3/P3) | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure

Interest: 3 (Assessing) | Patience: 3 | Target: Interest 5 for full blessing

Draw Steel Negotiation Mechanics: Each argument is a power roll using Reason, Intuition, or Presence. Motivation appeals = medium difficulty. Non-motivation arguments = difficult. Pitfalls = automatic failure (Interest −1, Patience −1).

With Patience 3 & Interest 3, the party needs Interest 5. Two Tier 2 motivation appeals or one Tier 2 + one Tier 3 reaches the goal. One pitfall is recoverable but dangerous. Two pitfalls = elves withdraw.

3 Motivations (medium difficulty):

  1. The Willing Sacrifice: Kaelen chose freely, not in despair. Grénvörðr asks: "Was this choice made in despair, or in duty?" — Party must show Kaelen understood the weight and walked the path. Special: If Kaelen speaks for herself on this motivation, grant an edge.
  2. The World's Future: If the locks fail, Grafvitnir walks, Helheim opens, everything ends. Grénvörðr asks: "What makes you believe this is the end of endings?" — Party must articulate this is not a cycle but a breaking of the wheel.
  3. The Old Compact: The First Keepers' covenant with the Green Heart elves. Requires Lore or creative reframing. Shifts Grénvörðr's entire demeanor: "She comes not as a stranger. She comes as the Compact promised."

3 Pitfalls (automatic failure):

  1. Urgency/impatience: "Time is not a resource we lack."
  2. Instrumentalizing the grove: Moss goes still, stream stops. "The grove is not a tool. It is alive."
  3. False authority: "Kings are dust. What do you command?"

Grénvörðr's Graduated Responses:

  • Interest rises: Ancient face becomes peaceful. Gold eyes brighten. "You speak truth. The roots confirm it."
  • Interest flat: Unmoved. "You have not convinced me."
  • Interest drops: Expression hardens. Light in tree dims. "That argument is beneath this moment."
  • Patience at 1: "We are running out of time. The roots are growing impatient."

Negotiation Outcomes

Full Success (Interest 5): Elves participate in transformation. Speak old Wyrd-language — binding is perfect (First Kindling density). Kaelen's transformation is slow — she has time to speak final words as herself. Root-Shard crystallizes at tree base. Possible final words: "Thank you for bringing me home." / "Take care of them. Please." / "It doesn't hurt. It's just... warm."

Partial Success (Interest 4): Elves accept with reservations. No old words spoken. Binding holds but requires tending in Acts 4–5. Transformation is faster — Kaelen has time for one word, maybe two. A name. A promise. But not a goodbye. Root-Shard still crystallizes.

Failure (Interest never reaches 4): Elves withdraw. Binding is weak — temporary (years/decades, not centuries). In Beat 26 Grafvitnir encounter, binding phase takes 5 rounds instead of 4. Lew's face becomes like stone. Root-Shard still crystallizes but is dimmer.

Part 3: The Transformation

Director Secret: Do not rush this. Do not add combat or complications. Give this moment the space it deserves.

What is happening mechanically: Kaelen is becoming the barrier substance itself — the dense membrane between the living world and Helheim. Her flame restores the barrier's density. Full blessing = First Kindling density (impenetrable). Without blessing = functional but fragile.

If a character was particularly close to Kaelen, they may feel warmth, a sense of presence, a feeling of being seen. This is not confirmation she still lives reachably — it is confirmation something of her remains, distributed and eternal.

Part 4: Lew's Departure

After the transformation settles, Lew announces his departure. He is going to walk the roots with Kaelen (via Wyrd-eye), gather allies: High-Hall dwarves, the elves, the Singing Islands, every debt and promise. He will arrive at the siege with the largest coalition in centuries.

Key lines: "I need you to hold Hrafnborg. When the dead come, when Grafvitnir pushes against the locks — you hold. You keep the shrine standing. You give me time."

Party Interaction:

  • If called out to: "I'll see you at Hrafnborg."
  • If stopped: "This is my choice. It was always going to be my choice."
  • If asked what Kaelen said: "Nothing I can repeat. But I understood."

Lew's departure is final. He will not rejoin until the later stages of the siege.

Part 5: Root-Shard of the Grove

Where Kaelen stood, a root-fragment has crystallized — warm, golden, humming. Champion wondrous quest item. Heart-Tracking (direction to Green Heart within 100mi), Kaelen-Sense (rough flame status), one-use Grafvitnir Stage 3 counter advance (then shard cracks and goes cold). Conditional on negotiation success.

Beat 22 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Part 2: Green Heart Audience NegotiationModerate (I3/P3)1 VP on success
Beat Total (Mandatory)1 VP

Beat 22 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Part 2: Green Heart Audience NegotiationModerate (I3/P3)1 VP on success
Beat Total (Mandatory)1 VP

What Happens Next

1–2 days travel west toward Hrafnborg. Party's role shifts from escort to defender. Beat 23: The Ashen Road (degraded terrain, refugees). Beat 24: Dreyfus confrontation. Beat 25: The siege of Hrafnborg.

The Root-Shard will grow warm during the siege — reminding the carrier: She is here. She is holding. You are not alone.

Beat 23 — The Ashen Road

Director Guide — Travel, Montage, Waystation Negotiation

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 60 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 12

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 60 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 12

Overview

The party travels ~120 miles from the Green Heart through the Ashen Woods to the Frostfen approach. This is the first time they travel without Lew. Everything they've relied on — pathfinding, scouting, morale — now falls entirely on the heroes. The emotional absence matters more than the mechanical one.

Midway through, they encounter Brynja Steinsdóttir and forty refugees at a waystation barn. Whether the refugees travel with the party changes the final approach to Hrafnborg.

Part 1 — Departure from the Green Heart

NOTE: Lew is gone. Don't dwell on it dramatically — the absence is practical. No one scouts ahead. No one reads animal trails. The party has to do everything themselves. Let the silence where Lew used to speak carry the weight.

The departure scene is brief. The Green Heart glow fades behind them. The woods close in.

Part 2 — The Ashen Woods (Recognition)

The party passes through territory they crossed in Act 1 — but degraded. This is about showing that the world has worsened while they were in the north.

DIRECTOR SECRET: Three specific landmarks have changed:
  • A hollow tree they sheltered near — now scored with claw scratches too high for any known animal.
  • A cairn that marked a crossroads — shifted, its guiding rune defaced by something deliberate.
  • Ice over a ford they crossed easily before — thinner now, with heavy two-legged tracks visible beneath.
These are signs of Grafvitnir's advance. Don't explain them — let players notice and wonder.

Part 3 — Montage Test: The Long Walk West

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Moderate | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure (2 rounds)

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Moderate | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure (2 rounds)

Moderate (SL 5 / FL 3) — 2 rounds, adjusted for 4 heroes

NOTE — NO LEW: Navigation challenges suffer a practical −1 edge (no automatic scouting or pathfinding). Make this felt, not punitive. The party can do this — they just have to work harder.

Round 1 — The Ashen Woods & Interior:

  • Navigate degraded paths (Reason) — landmarks obscured, Rot-twisted saplings
  • Manage supplies and rations (Might) — no one is hunting for them
  • Avoid disorientation (Intuition) — oppressive silence, easy to walk in circles
  • Spot other presences (Intuition) — something else is moving through these woods
  • Keep morale without Lew (Presence) — emotional labor to keep moving
  • Scout the way ahead (Agility) — check hazards, test ice, find easier paths

Round 1 Interlude: Smoke visible from the direction of Hrafnborg. Let the party react before continuing.

Round 2 — The Final Stretch:

  • Navigate Frostfen approach (Reason) — wetter ground, paths converge
  • Manage refugees' pace (Presence) — if refugees joined, keep 40 people moving
  • Scout siege ring from distance (Intuition) — undead organization becomes visible
  • Spot patrol patterns (Agility) — undead move in timed rotations
  • Keep group calm (Presence) — siege sounds audible, fear is real
  • Judge Frostfen crossing window (Intuition) — one window to cross safely
OUTCOMES:
Success: Standard arrival, everything proceeds. Hard success: +1 edge on first negotiation roll OR first Beat 24 Agility test.
Partial failure: Arrive exhausted (−1 Recovery each), −1 on first Brynja roll. Hard failure: Lost a day, −2 Recoveries, siege timeline tightens.

Part 4 — Waystation Encounter: Brynja Steinsdóttir

A barn on the edge of the interior lowlands. Forty refugees — families, children, elderly, traders. Brynja Steinsdóttir runs the camp with trade-warden efficiency. An old man recognizes the party from an earlier kindness.

NEGOTIATION — MODERATE:
Interest: 2 (Frightened) | Patience: 3
Motivations: Truth about Hrafnborg / Children's safety / Green Heart glow meaning
Pitfalls: Empty reassurance / Dismissiveness / Urgency trampling their need to decide
Full Success (Interest 4+): Refugees join, +1 to one Beat 25 Three-Crisis test
Partial (Interest 3): Refugees follow at distance, no bonus
Failure (Interest ≤2): Refugees decline, go their own way
DIRECTOR SECRET — DEMEANOR TELLS:
  • Interest rises: Shoulders relax. She nods — not warm, but acknowledging. "You've been honest. That's more than most."
  • Interest flat: Arms folded. Watching. "Tell me something I don't know."
  • Interest drops: Jaw tightens. She steps half a pace back. "We've heard enough promises."

Part 5 — Closing Scene

Whether the refugees join or not, the party continues toward Hrafnborg. The final read-aloud reveals the siege — smoke, walls, organized undead, the smell of cold iron. The Green Heart glow is behind them; Hrafnborg's walls are ahead.

Beat 23 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Part 3: The Long Walk West MontageModerate1 VP on success
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 1 VP

Beat 23 — Victory Point Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Part 3: The Long Walk West MontageModerate1 VP on success
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 1 VP

GM Notes & Callbacks

VIGMUND'S ADVOCACY OATH (Beat 13): If the party swore it, these forty refugees are the living embodiment of that promise — 40 more people depending on the party's word at Hrafnborg's council table. If they also swore the Silence Oath (Beat 17), the conflict sharpens: they know about a warm refuge and cannot speak of it.
THEMATIC NOTES:
  • Absence of Lew: The practical hole, not the dramatic one. Every navigation challenge should feel slightly harder without being unfair.
  • Refugee Subplot: If refugees join, they slow movement by 1 day (tightens Beat 24). They add emotional weight at the siege — more to protect, more to lose, but also seeds of recovery.
  • Foreshadowing Grafvitnir: The degraded landmarks, the organized undead, the two-legged tracks. Something bigger is moving. Don't name it yet — let the dread build.

Beat 24 — The Shape-Walker's Road

Director Guide — Dreyfus Boss Encounter, Djupá Montage, Hrafnborg Arrival, Level-Up to 7

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 61 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 13

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 61 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 13

Overview

Dreyfus, the Shape-Walker, intercepts the party on the road — alone, exhausted, and final. This is his last appearance in the campaign. After the confrontation, the party must use the Djupá valley to approach Hrafnborg before the siege ring closes. They arrive inside the walls for the first time since Act 1. Level-up to 7 at beat's end.

Part 1 — The Dreyfus Interception

DIRECTOR SECRET — BEAT 9 CALLBACK: Before running this scene, review your notes from Beat 9: which PC wounded Dreyfus, what weapon they used, where the blow landed. Dreyfus's degraded appearance here should carry the specific marks of that fight. If a PC drove a spear through his shoulder, his left arm doesn't shift cleanly. If they burned him, his raven form trails ash and singed feathers. The party should recognise their own handiwork.

Dreyfus stands in the road. Human form. Waiting. Gold unblinking eyes, skin the colour of old ivory, edges flickering like a candle in a draft. He is not the composed agent of Act 1 — he is exhausted, broken, and has stopped caring about precision.

He knows about Kaelen. He knows about the Green Heart. He attacks because it is what remains.

Part 2 — Boss Encounter: Dreyfus, L5 Elite

Victory Points — Type: Combat (Boss) | Difficulty: Hard (EV 444) | VP Award: 2 VP on victory

Victory Points — Type: Combat (Boss) | Difficulty: Hard (Level 5 Elite) | VP Award: 2 VP on victory

Stamina 64 | Speed 7 | Stability 2 | 2 turns/round

COMBAT FLOW:
Round 1: Precision and cruelty. Uses Void Pulse on the hero most likely making skill tests (targets confidence). Shifts to Raven form to scatter positioning, then back to humanoid for pressure.
Round 2: Villain Power depleted. Returns to human form. Faster, more direct — precision replaced by pure violence. Most dangerous here.
At 20 Stamina: Stops shifting. Can't maintain shapes. The party watches something degrade.
At 0 Stamina: Collapses into shadow. Flickering stops. For one moment — completely human. Older, more tired, a person who has been doing something terrible for a long time and has finally been stopped. No words. Eyes close. Gone.

Key Abilities:

  • Talon Strike: +4 vs Agility; 9 dmg, Slowed (save ends)
  • Void Pulse: +3 vs Intuition; 7 dmg, bane on next test
  • Shift Form (Villain Power): Wolf (+4 vs Might; 11 dmg, Prone), Raven (CB2, +3 vs Intuition; 5 dmg, Dazed, Speed 10 flying), Shadow (+3 vs Presence; 8 cold, −1 Recovery)
  • Flickering Body: Teleport 3 on 10+ damage

Wave Encounter: Dreyfus Final Duel (EV 444 — Hard)

Waves represent enemies Dreyfus has hidden in the shadow-road, certain he would not need them. He was wrong.

Wave 1 — The Ambush (EV 240)

Wave 2 — Last Reserves (EV 204)

Triggers when Dreyfus is reduced to ~20 Stamina or fewer.

MonsterCountEV ea.Subtotal

Part 3 — Aftermath & Trophy

Dreyfus's shadow-remains dissolve. No body. Allow the party space to process.

DREYFUS'S EYE (Conditional Loot): Where Dreyfus collapsed, a single golden eye-disc forms in the snow. Wondrous object. Wyrd-sight (1/encounter): Spend a Hero Token — see through deception, disguise, shape-change within CB5 for 1 minute. After use, the eye goes dark permanently. Not attuned.

Part 4 — The 'Little Do They Know' Beat

DIRECTOR SECRET — IT DOESN'T MATTER: The party may think clearing Dreyfus disrupts Grafvitnir's plans. It doesn't. Grafvitnir stopped needing Dreyfus the moment he positioned himself below Hrafnborg. He has been in the root-tunnels beneath the western wall for eleven days. The siege runs on old instructions — less coordinated without Dreyfus, but Grafvitnir doesn't need coordination. He needs time. All attention is up, watching the siege, while he comes from below. The real threat was never the spy network.

Dreyfus's Final Information (dying moment): "The Djupá valley. Northern approach. The siege ring hasn't reached it yet. You have perhaps twelve hours before it closes." — This is true. He was always an accurate reporter.

Part 5 — Hard Montage: The Djupá Approach

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test (Timed - failure triggers siege combat ring) | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test (Timed - failure triggers siege combat ring) | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure

Hard (SL 6 / FL 3) — 2 rounds, timed — urgency is the defining quality

Round 1 — The Valley: Navigate in falling light (Reason), Scout walls for siege scouts (Agility/Intuition), Move fast without alerting (Agility), Manage cold exposure (Might), Gather siege intelligence (Intuition), Tactical route decisions (Presence/Reason)

Round 1 Interlude: Hrafnborg visible. Ever-Ember's gold pulse in the great hall window. The siege ring is closing — eastern flank wheeling northward.

Round 2 — Final Approach: Time undead patrols (Reason/Intuition), Navigate valley mouth (Agility), Reach north gate (Presence/Might), Manage refugees (Presence), Spot movement windows (Intuition), Final push (Presence/Might)

OUTCOMES:
Hard Success (6+): Undetected entry. No alarm. Gap closes after them.
Success (4–5): Minor alarm, brief gate skirmish.
Failure (2–3): Pursued. Fighting at the gate, defenders hold the opening. 3 rounds of combat.
Hard Failure (0–1): Gap closes. Must fight through siege ring. Hard combat, multiple waves.

Part 6 — Hrafnborg Arrival & Harald

Inside the walls. The city is scarred — abandoned carts, blood on cobblestones, three fires burning. Smoke, arrows, siege equipment rhythm. But the Ever-Ember is still burning through the great hall windows. Gold. Warm.

Harald is on the walls. His face is haggard, ten years older than Act 1. When he sees the party, he nods. Once. The weight doesn't ease but he is still standing.

LEVEL-UP TO 7: The party levels up at the end of this beat. They will need every advantage for the Three-Crisis Siege (Beat 25) and Grafvitnir (Beat 26).

Beat 25 — The Last Flame

Director Guide — Three-Crisis Siege Montage, Söldís Arrival, Hildvarð's Heitfolk, Harald's Command

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 65 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 1
Level 5 begins - heroes levelled up completing Beat 24.

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 65 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 1
Level 5 begins - heroes levelled up completing Beat 24.

Overview

The party defends Hrafnborg against three simultaneous crises: the East Gate draugr tide, Rot Grubs undermining the western wall, and a Wight-Commander's battering ram at the main gate. This is an impossible triage — 4 heroes, 2 rounds, 8 tests total, but ~9 successes needed to hold all three crises. At least one will be neglected. The neglected crisis determines Grafvitnir's access in Beat 26.

Part 1 — Hrafnborg Assessment

The party is brought to Harald immediately. The King is on the walls — visible, organised, exhausted. He looks ten years older than Act 1. His beard is full of ash. His eyes are still clear but running on final reserves.

Harald's Briefing:

  • East Gate: Organised undead pressing in timed rotations. Gate braces beginning to splinter. Defenders rotating too fast, cold-bitten, exhausted.
  • West Wall: Reports from the tower basement — the stone sounds wrong. Hollow. Something is eating the foundation. The wall above is cracking. The whole tower might be compromised.
  • Main Gate: A Wight-Commander (draugr with intelligence) has assembled a bone-and-timber battering ram. Twelve draugr in synchronised movement. Two impacts already. Three more and the gate opens.
Note — Harald's Weight: Harald has held this city alone through the siege. He does not ask for sympathy — he asks for command structure. If the party hesitates, he waits patiently and repeats: "Tell me what to do." He will follow their orders because they are the heroes of this war, and he is the guardian of the walls.

Part 2 — Söldís Arrives

A cart arrives through a coastal route from the south — the last route before the siege ring tightens. Söldís Glóðfari drives it with complete conviction. She parks in the great hall courtyard, opens the back, and pulls out a clay pot — simple, sealed, ancient. The clay is cracked with age. The seal is wax and twine, wrapped so many times it has become sculptural.

She opens the pot. Inside: an ember. Twenty-two years old. Still burning. Still alive.

She sits next to the Ever-Ember shrine with the pot in her hands. She waits.

DIRECTOR SECRET — SÖLDÍS'S EMBER: The party does not know about Söldís's ember. No one told them she was coming. Lew never mentioned her. She is positioning herself for Beat 26. She has known, from the moment she lit her ember twenty-two years ago, that it would be needed for one specific moment. Do NOT telegraph her importance. Let the party see an eccentric old woman with a clay pot and wonder.

Part 3 — The Heitfolk Contingent & Hildvarð Bruneson

Hildvarð Bruneson emerges from the western tower basement — a deep-dwarf with grey-streaked hair, soot on face, and the competence of someone solving problems in bad conditions for a very long time. The Heitfolk arrived two days before the party through their tunnel system. Hildvarð has organised a defensive line, shoring up the foundation with Heitfolk stone-working expertise. But he cannot stop what is happening.

"The grubs came first. Rot-grubs, eating the foundation from inside. We've sealed what we can reach. But the deep tunnels run deeper than we can dig. Something followed the grubs. Something larger."

DIRECTOR SECRET — GRAFVITNIR'S ROUTE: The grubs are real. The tunnels are real. But Grafvitnir, rising from below, will follow these tunnels upward regardless of what the party does. The question is not whether he comes — he does. The question is how much the tunnels are widened by the party's neglect or narrowed by their effort. See Beat 26 for how the West Wall crisis result affects Grafvitnir's emergence speed.

Part 4 — The Three-Crisis Montage

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test (Extreme - 3 simultaneous crises) | Difficulty: Extreme | VP Award: 1 VP per crisis held (3+ successes each) - max 3 VP. No crisis can be fully won.

Victory Points — Type: Montage Test (Extreme - 3 simultaneous crises) | Difficulty: Extreme | VP Award: 1 VP per crisis held (3+ successes each) - max 3 VP. No crisis can be fully won.

CRITICAL — THIS MONTAGE CANNOT BE WON COMPLETELY: With 4 heroes over 2 rounds (8 tests), fully holding all three crises requires ~9 successes. Mathematically impossible. At least one crisis will receive insufficient attention. Track which crisis each hero's tests are assigned to.

Resources Available:

  • Nine able-bodied city guards (exhausted, trained)
  • Five Heitfolk (specialised in stone and structure)
  • Söldís (not a combatant, but present at the shrine)
  • The party (four heroes)
  • ~18 bodies total. Three simultaneous crises. Not enough.

Before each round: Each hero declares which crisis they address. Their test applies to that crisis's track only.

Crisis A: East Gate — The Draugr Tide

Round 1: Hold brace (Might), thin column from ramparts (Agility), rally defenders (Presence — Oath of Witness edge applies), identify wave pattern (Reason).

Round 2: Hold morale (Presence), brace failing gate (Might), scatter column (Agility).

Results: 0–1 = gate buckles, draugr in outer ward (fight through in B26). 2 = holds barely, minor incursion. 3+ = holds cleanly.

Contingent Combat: East Gate Breach (EV 548 — Hard)

Triggers if Crisis A fails (0-1 successes). The gate buckles; undead flood in.

Crisis B: West Wall — Rot Grubs in the Foundation

Round 1: Identify tunnelling pattern (Reason), shore up sections (Might), trace Rot origin (Intuition), collapse tunnels carefully (Agility).

Round 2: Predict failure points (Reason), more bracing (Might), find colony weak point (Intuition).

Results: 0–1 = fast violent emergence, no warning. 2 = one extra round warning. 3+ = clear warning, party can position.

DIRECTOR SECRET — ALWAYS CONTRIBUTES: Even with 3+ successes, the deep tunnels remain open. The surface sections can be sealed. Grafvitnir follows the deep passages upward regardless. What changes is the warning time the party gets before Stage 1 begins in Beat 26.

Crisis C: Main Gate — The Wight-Commander's Engine

Round 1: Disrupt ram carriers (Agility), drop debris (Might), identify Commander (Reason), intimidate Commander (Presence — Hard).

Round 2: Confront Commander (Presence), sabotage from below (Agility), heavy impact from above (Might).

Results: 0–1 = main gate cracks, draugr in inner ward. 2 = ram disrupted, gate damaged but holds. 3+ = ram destroyed, gate holds completely.

Triggers if Crisis C fails (0-1 successes). The Commander reaches the main gate unopposed.

Part 5 — Closing Transition

After Round 2 is complete, describe the final state of all three crises based on results. Then:

The sound from below the great hall stops. The silence is worse than the sound. The floor begins to crack. The Ever-Ember burns. Söldís is still there, clay pot in hands. The cracks spread from the center outward — in shapes that look almost like roots.

Beat 26 begins.

Key Narrative Themes

  1. Impossible triage: The party cannot win all three crises. They must choose. The consequences are immediate and visible in Beat 26.
  2. How outcomes feed Beat 26: The neglected crises become Grafvitnir's access points.
  3. Söldís's quiet heroism: She is not fighting. She is positioning herself for the moment when the flame goes out and must be restored.
  4. Defending a real place: Hrafnborg is not abstract — it is full of people. The party is defending all of them.

Beat 26 — Grafvitnir Rises

Three-stage boss encounter. Campaign climax. 10–12 rounds across 2–3 sessions.

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 68 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 4

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 68 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 4

Opening Based on Montage Outcomes

Choose the read-aloud version matching Beat 25 results:

Version A — All Three Crises Held (3+ successes each): The floor splits. Roots punch through like fists. Grafvitnir comes through the floor all at once, too fast, too large. Wolf-shaped, the size of a longhouse. Does not acknowledge the party. Goes immediately for the shrine.

Version B — One Crisis Failed, Two Held: The cracks are narrow. Grafvitnir emerges slower — forced to compress through tight passages. Thirty seconds of watching him force himself through the hole. Frustration in his growl. The passages were not wide enough.

Version C — Multiple Crises Failed (0–1 in two or more): The floor SHATTERS. The central section collapses inward. Roots erupt with such force they strike the ceiling. Grafvitnir ERUPTS — no hesitation, no squeezing. The party has seconds before he reaches the shrine.

Stage 1: The Grave-Wolf (L7 Solo, Huge)

Victory Points — Type: Combat (Boss - 3-stage final encounter) | Difficulty: Hard (Stage 1) / Hard-Extreme (Stage 2) / Extreme (Stage 3) | VP Award: 3 VP total on completing all stages

Stamina 200 | Speed 8 | 2 turns/round | 3 Villain Actions

Objective: Grafvitnir is trying to reach the Ever-Ember shrine, not kill the party. If uncontested, he walks past them. The party must be defenders.

Note: Primordial Resilience caps all single-source damage at 20. This is a major tactical constraint — the party cannot alpha-strike him down.

Round-by-Round Pacing:

  • Round 1: Charges shrine with both turns. VA1 (Howl of Binding) coordinates external undead. Party intercepts.
  • Round 2: Party learning his rhythm. VA2 (Lunge) breaks formation — moves full Speed + Bite, no free strikes.
  • Rounds 3–4: Low Stamina. VA3 (Rot Breath, CB3 +5 vs Might, 12 dmg, Slowed). All VAs spent. Push through.

Wave Encounter: Grafvitnir Stage 1 (EV 578, Hard)

Grafvitnir howls to call his fallen kin. Waves of draugr flood the Great Hall.

Key Mechanic — Undying Hunger: Regains 5 Stamina per turn unless the Ever-Ember is burning in the same zone. The flame IS the weapon.

Key Mechanic — Spectral Instability: Reduces all incoming damage by 3 (min 1). Predictable soak — the party always knows.

Director Secret — The Clock: Stage 2 is about managing time. If the party only deals damage, Undying Hunger means they're playing a losing game. They must keep the flame burning.

Key Narrative Moments (Stage 2)

Lew Arrives (when things look dire):

The north gate opens. Lew runs through — behind him: Heitfolk, Green Heart elves, survivors from the settlements. His amber eye blazing. He shouts: "She's growing! Half an hour, maybe less!" He looks at Grafvitnir for two seconds. Then at the shrine. Then at the party. "What do you need me to do?" Then he is moving.

Grafvitnir Extinguishes the Flame: One thin, precise tendril wraps around the shrine. The flame goes out. The hall goes dark — all at once. The cold comes in immediately. Not weather-cold. The cold of a door into somewhere that has never been warm.

Söldís's Act:

Director Secret: This is the emotional climax. Söldís opens the clay pot she has carried for twenty-two years. She tilts it over the shrine. The flame IGNITES — fills the shrine, rises up the walls, warm and huge and unmistakably alive in the way only something kept alive by absolute refusal can be. Söldís sets the pot down. She does not look upset. She looks like a woman who has completed a task she began decades ago.

Stage Transition: Grafvitnir's howl shakes the city. He grows. Roots pull more mass from the ground. The ceiling breaks apart — sky visible above. He is no longer any shape. Through the hole: the Green Heart, blazing, growing stronger. For the first time, Grafvitnir is uncertain.

Stage 3: The Hunger Made Manifest (L8 Solo, Titanic)

Stamina 150 (fresh) | Fills space | 2 turns/round | 3 Villain Actions

CRITICAL — 4-ROUND SURVIVAL COUNTER: The party cannot kill Grafvitnir. They must SURVIVE him for 4 rounds. The Green Heart seal closes automatically at Round 4. Victory is guaranteed if they hold on.

Round Counter:

  • Round 1: Green Heart blazing gold. Barrier thickening. Unnatural cold retreats from flagstones.
  • Round 2: Light brighter. Warm on faces. Grafvitnir screaming — barrier densifying around him.
  • Round 3: Light intense. Gold, blazing, rewriting the sky. Cold being burned away. Barrier nearly restored.
  • Round 4: Light goes WHITE. Barrier slams shut. Read the Sealing read-aloud.

Villain Action Pacing: VA1 (World-Ending Swipe, CB5, 15 dmg, Prone) opens with overwhelming force. VA2 (Despair Made Flesh, strips Hero Tokens + Frightened) is psychological escalation. VA3 (Root Storm, 3 targets × 12 dmg, Slowed) is targeted destruction before the seal closes.

Prison Madness: Cannot be Taunted, Frightened, or reasoned with. He is beyond psychology.

The Sealing

Light goes white — clean, absolute, unstoppable. Roots RECOIL. Grafvitnir is simply pulled downward. Cracks seal from the inside out. Undead outside stop — like puppets when the hand opens. The unnatural cold retreats. Natural Svellheim winter remains. The Ever-Ember burns. Kaelen's work burns in the east. Hrafnborg is warm.

Post-Combat Aftermath

Give the party space. The great hall is devastated but whole. The Ever-Ember burns gold and steady. Söldís sits by the shrine, empty clay pot in hand, smiling and crying. Lew trembles, looking east toward the Green Heart — toward Kaelen. Harald watches from the walls as the siege lines break apart.

Note: This is the climax of the entire campaign. Let the party sit with this victory. Answer questions. Let them interact with NPCs. Let the weight of what they've done settle. They have won.

Beat 27 — Epilogue

Resolution. NPC vignettes. Campaign close. 1 session. Quiet. Earned.

Beat 27 — Victory Point Summary

The Morning After

The siege is over. The outer wards are littered with fallen undead — now just bodies returning to the stillness they should have kept. People are emerging from shelters. Someone is laughing. Fires are being built — not siege-fires, ordinary warming fires.

Tone Note: This beat is about aftermath and the specific texture of victory that doesn't feel like victory until you stop and look around. Stage whichever vignettes feel right for the table — none are mandatory.

NPC Vignettes

Harald: The King on the Walls

Harald stands at the Ever-Ember shrine. Not praying — just standing there. Looks at the flame, the scorch-marks, the whole flagstones. After a long time, he nods once. Then turns away and starts working.

If the party speaks with him: "You held. The city held. That's what matters." About Kaelen: "Then she's not lost to us. She's doing what needs to be done."

Note: Harald will govern well for the rest of his life because he has now seen exactly what Hrafnborg is worth defending.

Söldís: The Ember-Trader at Rest

Her clay pot sits on a shelf, empty, ordinary. She sits next to it one morning. Then she gets up, loads her cart, assembles her caravan. Three new people from Hrafnborg join her.

If the party speaks with her: "I did what needed doing. My flame and the Ever-Ember — they belonged together. Now they are." She offers each party member a small carved token. "You held when holding was the only thing that mattered."

Director Secret: For the first time in twenty-two years, Söldís travels light. Her caravan grows — not with embers, but with people and goods and news. Her arc is complete.

The Green Heart: Kaelen's New Home

If the party travels east: the grove is changed. Trees lit gold from within. Snow melts within fifty feet of the central tree — clear bare ground where grass might grow in spring.

Grénvörðr: "She is learning. To be a flame. To be a root. To be both and neither. Do not mistake silence for absence."

The central tree's light pulses in response to the party's presence. Not speech — communication in warmth, rhythm, flame-pulse: I remember you.

On Yggdrasil: "The Rot is still there. Below her. But Yggdrasil is no longer dying. She anchors it. She feeds it light. In three hundred years, perhaps longer, Yggdrasil might heal."

Lew: The Wanderer's Return

Lew stays in Hrafnborg for several days. Eats, sleeps, helps with wall repairs. His amber eye glows steadier. His attention turns east.

One evening, a party member finds him on the north wall, looking east. After a few days, he's gone. Left his spare pack, his cooking knife, and a note: "Tell them I came back."

Director Secret — Lew at the Green Heart: If the party follows, they find him kneeling at the inner grove. One hand on the roots. Both eyes open — one dark, one amber and blazing. Speaking quietly. A vow, or a promise. When he stands, he touches the eye once — gently, the way you'd touch a scar that saved your life — and nods. The tree-light pulses in answer. The arc completes: the Keeper-marked man reaching the woman he was always going to guide to this place.

The Settlements: Recovery Begins

Settlements the party helped are now recovering. Supplies moving between settlements and the city. Trade beginning. The world starting to believe it might not die after all.

Thyra Blackhand: "You did what you said you would. You gave us a reason to believe."

Final Image

Read the final read-aloud. On quiet nights, from the north wall, people see a light moving in the Green Heart. Not a torch — steady, following no path, never hurries. It pulses. Like a heartbeat answering another heartbeat across a room. Nobody has gone to look for it. Lew has.

Campaign Reflection (Optional)

If the table wants a debrief: questions about the world, NPCs, what-ifs. Reflection on the journey. Discussion of themes. Moments that mattered.

Note: The party did not save the world by becoming extraordinary. They saved it by being the vehicle and protection — getting the right people to the right place, defending the things that needed defending, holding the line when holding was all that was left. Let them sit with that.
